claim.statement: No 3-coloring of [1,...,13] avoids both monochromatic Schur triples x+y=z and monochromatic 3-term arithmetic progressions.
claim.negation_of: a 3-coloring of [1,...,13] with no monochromatic Schur triple and no monochromatic 3-term arithmetic progression

rung.name: verified-checker-program
trusted_base: checkProofArr_unsat (Lean-proved sound: checkProofArr f steps = true -> Unsat f)

meta.format: flat space-separated DIMACS ints consumed by parseCert
meta.cross_reference: same finite claim as hybrid-schur-vdw-3color-le-13 (R3 exhaustive Python recomputation)

encoder.variable_scheme: point-major, zero-based colors, one-based DIMACS variables

provenance.pipeline: deterministic hybrid CNF encoder -> prefix-blocking RUP proof generator -> rup_check self-check -> lratcheck

one_directional: The Lean-proved checker can never falsely certify a parsed CNF as UNSAT; a parser or proof-generation bug only causes refusal. The atlas binds exact cert bytes by sha256, cross-checks parsed clause/step counts, and regenerates the hybrid Schur/vdW CNF from the declared encoder spec before invoking lratcheck. This is a new atlas-certified finite result (also Lean-formalized as hybrid13_no_avoiding_coloring in lean-verification-journey), not a literature-priority claim.
